applicable_bylaws: BDA Revised Master Plan 2031 (Draft) – Zoning Regulations

planning_zones.Zone_A: Area falling within the Outer Ring Road (ORR)
planning_zones.Zone_B: Area outside the ORR and up to the conurbation limit
planning_zones.Zone_C: Area beyond the conurbation limit and up to the Local Planning Area boundary – Agricultural zone

land_use_zones: C1 – Commercial Category 1 (neighbourhood shops, small offices)
C2 – Commercial Category 2 (retail, nursing homes, fuel stations)
C3 – Commercial Category 3 (offices, malls, hotels, hospitals)
C4 – Commercial Category 4 (warehouses, wholesale, junk yards)
C5 – Commercial Category 5 (agro mandis, heavy goods markets)
PSP1 – Public & Semi-Public Cat-1 (local amenities, creches)
PSP2 – Public & Semi-Public Cat-2 (police, post offices, primary schools)
PSP3 – Public & Semi-Public Cat-3 (hospitals, colleges, government offices)
PSP4 – Public & Semi-Public Cat-4 (airports and ancillary uses)
PU  – Public Utilities (permitted in all zones)
_meta.notes: FAR has two components: Base FAR and Allowable-against-TDR FAR. Total Maximum = Base + TDR. All values sourced from BDA RMP 2031 Draft Tables 6, 7, 12, 13, 17, 20, 22. VERIFY all fields against the official gazette before use in production.

far._note: FAR by zone x road width and plot size. RMP 2031 uses a plot-size + road-width matrix. Road width keys represent the UPPER boundary of the bracket in metres (9999 = 30.5m and above).
far._status: VERIFY – sourced from Tables 6, 7, 12, 13, 17, 20, 22 of BDA RMP 2031 Draft.

R._note: Residential – Planning Zone A (Table 6) and Zone B (Table 7). FAR varies by plot size AND road width. Values below are Base FAR / Total Maximum FAR per planning zone.

zone_A._note: Planning Zone A (within Outer Ring Road). Rows = plot size (sqm), cols = road width upper bracket (m).

zone_B._note: Planning Zone B (outside ORR to conurbation limit). Rows = plot size (sqm), cols = road width upper bracket (m).

R._large_plot_note: For plots above 20,000 sqm, non-residential layout / development plan norms apply. Refer BDA RMP 2031 Table 8.

C._note: Commercial zones C1-C5. Plot size threshold for individual plot vs. development plan = 12,000 sqm. FAR governed by Table 12 (Zone A) and Table 13 (Zone B).

zone_A._source: Table 12 – FAR and Ground Coverage for Commercial in Planning Zone A (up to 12,000 sqm)

zone_B._source: Table 13 – FAR and Ground Coverage for Commercial in Planning Zone B (up to 12,000 sqm)

C._note_C1_C2: C1 and C2 are the lower-order commercial categories. FAR governed by the same Table 12/13 applicable to the road width.
C._note_C3_C5: C3-C5 are higher-order commercial categories. Same FAR table applies; road width eligibility determines which category can be operated.
C._note_hi_tech_bonus: For Industrial Hi-Tech (I-2) plots, FAR = Base Commercial FAR + 0.25 over the prescribed Base FAR for commercial use.
C._large_plot_note: For plots above 12,000 sqm, non-residential layout/development plan norms apply per BDA RMP 2031 Tables 23 and 24.

I._note: Industrial zones I1-I5 (other than Hi-Tech). FAR governed by Table 17 of RMP 2031. Also keyed by plot size.
I._source: Table 17 – Setbacks and Coverage for Industrial Buildings

flatted_factory._note: Special norms for flatted factories (industrial stacked buildings)

PSP._note: Public and Semi-Public zones PSP1-PSP4. FAR governed by Table 20 of RMP 2031.
PSP._source: Table 20 – FAR and Ground Coverage in Public and Semi-public Zone
PSP._note_ancillary: Ancillary uses shall not exceed 20% of site area.

T._note: Transportation and Communication zones T1-T4. FAR governed by Table 22 of RMP 2031.
T._source: Table 22 – FAR and Ground Coverage in Transportation and Communication Zone
T._note_MLCP: For Multi-Level Car Parking (MLCP) as independent activity, there is no FAR or height limitation subject to fire safety and airport clearances.

AG._note: Agricultural zone (Planning Zone C). No FAR prescribed for agricultural uses.

setbacks._note: All values in metres. Two tables govern setbacks: Table 1 (front setback by road width) and Table 2 (all-round setbacks by building height and plot area).
setbacks._source: BDA RMP 2031, Section 4.5, Table 1 and Table 2

setbacks_by_building_height._note: Table 2 – Minimum all-round setbacks by height and plot area.

setbacks.height_limit_rule: Maximum building height shall not exceed 1.5 x (abutting road width + front setback), subject to a maximum front open space of 16m.
setbacks.corner_plot_note: Both sides facing road shall be treated as front; FAR computed on the wider road.
setbacks.multi_building_distance_m: Distance between buildings on same site = 0.5 x height of tallest building, or 16m, whichever is lower.

setback_projections.balcony_projection_rule: 1/3rd of setback or 1.2m (first floor); max 1.75m beyond second floor – whichever is less
setback_projections.portico_max_size_m: 3m x 4.5m at ground floor only; 1m clearance from boundary

exempted_from_height_calc: AC chiller plants, solar panels, radio antennas on terrace (up to 3.5m)
Lift machine rooms (up to 3.5m above terrace)
Internal open space rain cover (up to 2m)
height.height_formula: Max height = 1.5 x (road_width + front_setback), max 16m front open space

fire_safety.general_rule: NBC 2016 Part IV applies to all buildings.
fire_safety.non_residential_rule: All non-residential buildings with BUA above 5,000 sqm require firefighting arrangements per Authority directions irrespective of height.

basement.site_size_exclusion: Car parking not permitted in basement if site area < 200 sqm and depth < 20m
permitted_uses: Car parking (primary use – up to 5 levels)
Dark rooms for X-ray and light-sensitive material storage
Air conditioning handling units and utilities/services

balcony.projection_rule: Lesser of 1/3rd of setback on that side or the maximum limits above

lifts.high_rise_service_lift: All high-rise buildings shall mandatorily provide at least one service lift
lifts.service_lift_combined_threshold: Buildings with fewer than 24 units OR less than 2,400 sqm BUA may use combined passenger + service lift

residential_multi_dwelling.du_above_120sqm: 1 car per DU + 1 additional car per 120 sqm above threshold

requirements_by_use.residential_single_dwelling: 1 car per 100 sqm BUA (mandatory for plots >= 90 sqm)
requirements_by_use.retail_shops_malls: 1 car per 50 sqm + 1 lorry per 1,000 sqm
requirements_by_use.industrial: 1 car per 100 sqm + 1 lorry per 1,000 sqm
requirements_by_use.it_bt_office_above_20000sqm: 1 car per 50 sqm + 1 bus per 1,500 sqm
requirements_by_use.hotel_star: 1 car per 2 rooms + 1 car per 50 sqm (excl. room area) for visitors
requirements_by_use.educational: 1 car per 150 sqm + 1 bus per 120 students + drop-off lane

exemptions: Buildings with total floor area up to 100 sqm are exempted from parking

items: Service areas: electrical substation/panel room, generator, pump room, AC plant room, solid waste management, fire control room, security/CCTV room
Refuge areas in high-rise buildings (per fire safety norms)
Separate public toilet block in non-residential complex
Building management/society room up to 40 sqm
Architectural features not usable for living
Parking areas including driveways and ramps
Swimming pools and attached toilets (common use)
Lift wells and lift machine rooms
Sewerage Treatment Plant and pump rooms
Stilt parking floor (if fully open on all sides)
Basement floors used for parking and services only

accessibility._trigger: Public and semi-public buildings with covered area ≥ 300 sqm are mandated. Recommended for all buildings.

access_path.note: From plot entry and surface parking to building entry

ramp.note: At least one entrance must have ramp approach to plinth level

toilet.note: One special WC per set of toilets with wash basin for handicapped persons

required_at: beginning/end of walkway with vehicular traffic

signage.braille_board: Required on walls at suitable height — approachable closely
signage.note: Contrasting colour, properly illuminated; no protruding signs that obstruct walking

corner_plot.note: Both corners must be rounded off or chamfered at the intersection

exceptions: Women's hostels — may exceed standard height with special permission
compound_wall.licence_fee: Rs. 1 per running metre for compound wall licence

ToD._note: Transit Oriented Development zones around metro stations – additional FAR permissible per Section 4.18.
